remediation: CycleService.Check: the 033 bug-only tolerance in BuildReleaseTrain (a null cycle-state yields an empty feature half + consults the injected bug members) was never extended to the Check() chokepoint — the release-train block there is guarded by 'state is not null', so a bug-only repo never reaches it and the transitive-prerequisite loop marks specify..drift-review 'missing'. Add a release-scoped null-state branch that delegates readiness ENTIRELY to the bug-aware release train (BuildReleaseTrain(null)): a valid train (>=1 test-passed, unreleased bug) passes with a stand-in 'ready' result; an empty/invalid one fails closed on the train's own blocker. Scoped to target.Id=='release' only, so feature-stage checks are unchanged. Proven on the real ergon repo (bugs 004+005) — the check now passes without fabricating a cycle-state.
summary: hx doti cycle check --stage release is not bug-only-aware: on a repo with no .doti/cycle-state.json it marks all 8 feature stages 'missing' and fails, a false blocker that misleads agents into fabricating a feature cycle for a bug-only release.
